name: Minimal
usedFor: Board slides, all-hands, live keynote, opening/closing slides
character: Almost poster-like. One dominant element. Audience grasps the point in under 2 seconds.

name: Clean
usedFor: Executive steering, strategy off-site, investor roadshow, transformation steering
dataGranularity: 3-5 key data points, one decimal max
annotationDepth: Headline + 1-3 annotations on chart + optional implication
character: One clear chart or diagram dominates. A few annotations highlight the key finding. Spacious but substantive.

name: Structured
usedFor: QBR body, client pitch detail, pre-reads, vendor review
annotationDepth: Headline + 2-4 callouts + implication sentences + methodology note
character: Tells a complete story without a speaker. Chart is annotated, implications stated, sources visible.

name: Detailed
usedFor: Deal review, regulatory briefing body, analytical pre-reads, working session detail
dataGranularity: Full dataset visible, exact figures, multiple series
annotationDepth: Headline + narrative paragraph + multiple callouts + full methodology
sourceCompleteness: Full source chain + date + base + methodology + caveats + definitions
character: Information-rich but rigorously organized. Clear visual hierarchy prevents chaos.

name: Dense / Appendix
usedFor: Appendix slides, diligence backup, regulatory evidence, methodology documentation
audience: L5 (Manager/analyst), referenced by L3-L4 on demand
dataGranularity: Full tables, all rows, exact precision
annotationDepth: Full methodology, all assumptions, edge case notes
character: Resembles a well-formatted report page. Structured with clear headline, labeled sections. Never a raw data dump.
